title: తండ్రి మాట — ధర్మం బాట
moral: ఇచ్చిన మాట నిలబెట్టుకోవడమే గొప్ప ధర్మం అనిపిస్తుంది, అది కష్టమైనా సరే.
main_character: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The ancient city of Ayodhya, bustling with life and grand palaces, later transitioning to the serene yet wild banks of the river Ganga and the deep forests. The setting shifts from royal grandeur to natural wilderness. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: అయోధ్య నగరం అప్పుడు పండుగ శోభతో మెరిసిపోతోంది, ఎక్కడ చూసినా సందడే సందడి. లక్ష్మణుడు సంతోషంతో ఉప్పొంగిపోతూ, సీతమ్మ ప్రశాంతంగా నవ్వుతూ, శ్రీరాముని పట్టాభిషేకానికి జరుగుతున్న ఏర్పాట్లను చూస్తున్నారు. పూల సువాసన, అగరుబత్తీల ధూపం గాలిలో కలిసిపోయి, మనసుకు ఎంతో హాయిగా ఉంది. లక్ష్మణుడు ఉత్సాహంగా, "శ్రీరాముడు పట్టాభిషిక్తుడవుతున్నాడు! ఎంత అద్భుతం!" అని కేక పెట్టాడు. ఒక్కసారిగా — అన్నీ ఆగిపోయాయి. ఇంతలో, రాజభవనం నుండి ఒక కంఠం, రాజుగారి పిలుపు అంటూ వినిపించింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The three characters stand amidst the festive preparations, their joyful expressions suddenly freezing as a voice calls from the palace. The air, previously filled with the scent of flowers and incense, now hangs heavy with an unexpected silence. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Bright morning sun streaming through palace arches, illuminating golden decorations and vibrant silks, festive and warm.
scenes.scene_visual: The three characters stand amidst the festive preparations, their joyful expressions suddenly freezing as a voice calls from the palace. The air, previously filled with the scent of flowers and incense, now hangs heavy with an unexpected silence.
scenes.text_te_en: Ayodhya Nagaram Appudu Panduga Shobhato Merisipotondi, Ekkada Chusinaa Sandade Sandadi. Lakshmanudu Santoshanto Uppongipotu, Sitamma Prashaantangaa Navvutu, Shriraamuni Pattaabhishekaaniki Jarugutunna Erpaatlanu Chustunnaaru. Pula Suvaasana, Agarubattila Dhupam Gaalilo Kalisipoyi, Manasuku Ento Haayigaa Undi. Lakshmanudu Utsaahangaa, "Shriraamudu Pattaabhishiktudavutunnaadu! Enta Adbhutan!" Ani Keka Pettaadu. Okkasaarigaa — Anni Aagipoyaayi. Intalo, Raajabhavanam Nundi Oka Kanthan, Raajugaari Pilupu Antu Vinipinchindi.
scenes.text_en: The city of Ayodhya was sparkling with festive joy, bustling with activity everywhere. Lakshmana was brimming with happiness, and Sita was smiling peacefully, as they watched the preparations for Sri Rama's coronation. The sweet scent of flowers and the smoke of incense filled the air, bringing great comfort to their hearts. Lakshmana excitedly shouted, "Sri Rama is being crowned! How wonderful!" Suddenly — everything stopped. Just then, a voice from the royal palace announced, "The King calls!"

scenes.text: ఆ పిలుపు వినగానే, ఆ సంతోషమంతా ఎక్కడికో మాయమైపోయింది. దశరథ మహారాజు కైకేయితో మాట్లాడుతూ, ఆయన చేతులు వణికిపోతున్నాయి — పట్టుకున్న వస్త్రాలు నలిగిపోయాయి. ఆమె ముఖం మాత్రం కఠినంగా, ఏ మాత్రం చలించకుండా ఉంది. మహారాజు గొంతు వణుకుతూ, "ఈ మాటలు నేను ఎలా పలకగలను, కైకేయీ?" అని అడిగాడు. ఆయన గుండెలో — పెను తుఫాను రేగింది. తనకిచ్చిన రెండు వరాలు ఇప్పుడు రాముని అడవికి పంపమని, భరతుడికి పట్టాభిషేకం చేయమని అడుగుతోంది. ఆ క్షణంలోనే, ఒక దూత రాముని పిలవడానికి బయలుదేరాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The old king stands before the queen, his hands trembling so violently that the clothes he holds are crumpled. The queen's face remains hard and unmoving, showing no trace of emotion. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Dasharatha — a well-known Hindu mythology figure, depicted accurately per canonical tradition. Dim, melancholic light in Dasaratha's private chamber, shadows clinging to the corners, heavy and sorrowful.
scenes.scene_visual: The old king stands before the queen, his hands trembling so violently that the clothes he holds are crumpled. The queen's face remains hard and unmoving, showing no trace of emotion.
scenes.text_te_en: Aa Pilupu Vinagaane, Aa Santoshamantaa Ekkadiko Maayamaipoyindi. Dasharatha Mahaaraaju Kaikeyito Maatlaadutu, Aayana Chetulu Vanikipotunnaayi — Pattukunna Vastraalu Naligipoyaayi. Aame Mukham Maatram Kathinangaa, E Maatram Chalinchakundaa Undi. Mahaaraaju Gontu Vanukutu, "I Maatalu Nenu Elaa Palakagalanu, Kaikeyi?" Ani Adigaadu. Aayana Gundelo — Penu Tuphaanu Regindi. Tanakichchina Rendu Varaalu Ippudu Raamuni Adaviki Pampamani, Bharatudiki Pattaabhishekam Cheyamani Adugutondi. Aa Kshananlone, Oka Duta Raamuni Pilavadaaniki Bayaluderaadu.
scenes.text_en: As soon as that call was heard, all the joy seemed to vanish. King Dasharatha was speaking with Kaikeyi, his hands trembling so much that the clothes he held were crumpled. Her face, however, remained stern and completely unmoved. The King's voice quivered as he asked, "How can I utter these words, Kaikeyi?" A great storm raged in his heart. She was now asking for the two boons he had promised her: to send Rama to the forest and to crown Bharata. At that very moment, a messenger was sent to call Rama.

scenes.text: దూత మాట విని శ్రీరాముడు తండ్రి గదిలోకి అడుగు పెట్టగానే, అక్కడి వాతావరణం ఎంతో బరువుగా అనిపించింది. దశరథ మహారాజు బాధతో మాట్లాడలేకపోయారు — ఆయన కళ్ళు తల దించుకున్నాయి. కైకేయి మాత్రం స్థిరమైన గొంతుతో, రామునికి అడవికి వెళ్లమని చెప్పింది, పదనాలుగు సంవత్సరాలు. రాముని ముఖంలో ఏమాత్రం మార్పు లేదు, ఆయన ప్రశాంతంగా ఉన్నారు. "పితృవాక్య పరిపాలన నా ధర్మం," అంటూ రాముడు ధైర్యంగా పలికారు. ఆయన అడుగులు — ధర్మం వైపు సాగాయి. వెంటనే అడవికి వెళ్ళడానికి సిద్ధం కావాలని నిర్ణయించుకున్నారు.
scenes.image_prompt: The prince stands calmly before his father and the queen, his face showing no change as he accepts the command to go to the forest. The old king's eyes are cast down in sorrow, unable to speak, while the queen speaks with a steady voice. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Dasharatha — a well-known Hindu mythology figure, depicted accurately per canonical tradition. The chamber remains dimly lit, but a faint, almost ethereal glow surrounds Rama, highlighting his serene composure amidst the gloom.
scenes.scene_visual: The prince stands calmly before his father and the queen, his face showing no change as he accepts the command to go to the forest. The old king's eyes are cast down in sorrow, unable to speak, while the queen speaks with a steady voice.
scenes.text_te_en: Duta Maata Vini Shriraamudu Tandri Gadiloki Adugu Pettagaane, Akkadi Vaataavaranam Ento Baruvugaa Anipinchindi. Dasharatha Mahaaraaju Baadhato Maatlaadalekapoyaaru — Aayana Kallu Tala Dinchukunnaayi. Kaikeyi Maatram Sthiramaina Gontuto, Raamuniki Adaviki Vellamani Cheppindi, Padanaalugu Sanvatsaraalu. Raamuni Mukhanlo Emaatram Maarpu Ledu, Aayana Prashaantangaa Unnaaru. "Pitrrivaakya Paripaalana Naa Dharman," Antu Raamudu Dhairyangaa Palikaaru. Aayana Adugulu — Dharmam Vaipu Saagaayi. Ventane Adaviki Velladaaniki Siddham Kaavaalani Nirnayinchukunnaaru.
scenes.text_en: When Sri Rama heard the messenger and stepped into his father's room, the air felt very heavy. King Dasharatha was too sad to speak — his eyes were cast down. Kaikeyi, however, with a steady voice, told Rama that he must go to the forest for fourteen years. There was no change on Rama's face; he remained calm. "Upholding my father's word is my duty," Rama said bravely. His steps — moved towards righteousness. He immediately decided to prepare to go to the forest.

scenes.text: రాముడు లక్ష్మణుడికి, సీతమ్మకి అడవికి వెళ్లాల్సిన విషయాన్ని ప్రశాంతంగా వివరించారు. లక్ష్మణుడు కోపంతో తన కత్తి పిడిని పట్టుకున్నాడు — అతని చేతి వేళ్ళు తెల్లబడ్డాయి. కానీ వెంటనే రాముడిని అనుసరించాలని నిర్ణయించుకున్నాడు. సీతమ్మ రాముని వైపు చూసి, స్థిరమైన స్వరంతో, "మీరు లేని స్వర్గం నాకు వద్దు!" అని చెప్పింది. ఆ క్షణంలో — వారి బంధం మరింత బలపడింది. ముగ్గురూ కలిసి, రాజ వస్త్రాలను తీసివేసి, అడవిలో ధరించే నార చీరలు కట్టుకోవడానికి సిద్ధమయ్యారు.
scenes.image_prompt: The younger brother grips the hilt of his sword in anger, his knuckles turning white, while the princess looks steadfastly at the prince. All three characters then begin to shed their royal garments, preparing to don simple forest attire. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. A soft, warm light in their personal chambers, emphasizing the intimacy and strength of their bond, a ray of hope amidst the sorrow.
scenes.scene_visual: The younger brother grips the hilt of his sword in anger, his knuckles turning white, while the princess looks steadfastly at the prince. All three characters then begin to shed their royal garments, preparing to don simple forest attire.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu Lakshmanudiki, Sitammaki Adaviki Vellaalsina Vishayaanni Prashaantangaa Vivarinchaaru. Lakshmanudu Kopanto Tana Katti Pidini Pattukunnaadu — Atani Cheti Vellu Tellabaddaayi. Kaani Ventane Raamudini Anusarinchaalani Nirnayinchukunnaadu. Sitamma Raamuni Vaipu Chusi, Sthiramaina Svaranto, "Miru Leni Svargam Naaku Vaddu!" Ani Cheppindi. Aa Kshananlo — Vaari Bandham Marinta Balapadindi. Mugguru Kalisi, Raaja Vastraalanu Tisivesi, Adavilo Dharinche Naara Chiralu Kattukovadaaniki Siddhamayyaaru.
scenes.text_en: Rama calmly explained to Lakshmana and Sita that they had to go to the forest. Lakshmana angrily gripped the hilt of his sword — his knuckles turned white. But he quickly decided to follow Rama. Sita looked at Rama and said with a steady voice, "I don't want heaven without you!" In that moment — their bond grew even stronger. All three together took off their royal clothes and prepared to wear the simple bark garments worn in the forest.

scenes.text: నార చీరలు కట్టుకున్న రాముడు, సీతమ్మ, లక్ష్మణుడు అయోధ్య ప్రజలకు, కన్నీళ్లతో ఉన్న దశరథ మహారాజుకు వీడ్కోలు చెప్పడానికి నగర ద్వారం వద్ద నిలబడ్డారు. గాలిలో ప్రజల నిశ్శబ్దపు కన్నీళ్లు బరువుగా అలుముకున్నాయి — ఒక విషాదం మేఘంలా కమ్ముకుంది. రాముడు ప్రశాంతంగా, "మా తండ్రి మాట నెరవేర్చడమే నా కర్తవ్యం," అని పలికారు. అయోధ్య — మౌనంగా ఏడ్చింది. వారు నగరం నుండి బయలుదేరగానే, దశరథ మహారాజు ఆ దృశ్యాన్ని చూడలేక, బాధతో అక్కడే కుప్పకూలిపోయారు.
scenes.image_prompt: The prince, princess, and younger brother stand at the city gate in their simple forest clothes, bidding farewell to the weeping king and the silent, sorrowful crowd. As they depart, the old king collapses to the ground, unable to bear the sight. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Dasharatha — a well-known Hindu mythology figure, depicted accurately per canonical tradition. Hazy, sorrowful morning light over the city gates, casting long shadows, reflecting the mood of departure and loss.
scenes.scene_visual: The prince, princess, and younger brother stand at the city gate in their simple forest clothes, bidding farewell to the weeping king and the silent, sorrowful crowd. As they depart, the old king collapses to the ground, unable to bear the sight.
scenes.text_te_en: Naara Chiralu Kattukunna Raamudu, Sitamma, Lakshmanudu Ayodhya Prajalaku, Kannillato Unna Dasharatha Mahaaraajuku Vidkolu Cheppadaaniki Nagara Dvaaram Vadda Nilabaddaaru. Gaalilo Prajala Nishshabdapu Kannillu Baruvugaa Alumukunnaayi — Oka Vishaadam Meghanlaa Kammukundi. Raamudu Prashaantangaa, "Maa Tandri Maata Neraverchadame Naa Kartavyan," Ani Palikaaru. Ayodhya — Maunangaa Edchindi. Vaaru Nagaram Nundi Bayaluderagaane, Dasharatha Mahaaraaju Aa Drrishyaanni Chudaleka, Baadhato Akkade Kuppakulipoyaaru.
scenes.text_en: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana, now dressed in simple bark clothes, stood at the city gate to bid farewell to the people of Ayodhya and King Dasharatha, who was in tears. The silent tears of the people hung heavy in the air — a cloud of sadness covered everything. Rama calmly said, "Fulfilling my father's promise is my duty." Ayodhya — wept silently. As they left the city, King Dasharatha, unable to bear the sight, collapsed there in sorrow.

scenes.text: గంగా నది ఒడ్డుకు చేరుకున్న రాముడు, సీతమ్మ, లక్ష్మణుడు ఒక చిన్న పడవలో నదిని దాటడం మొదలుపెట్టారు. ఆ చిన్న పడవకు నీటి తాకిడి తప్ప, వేరే ఏ శబ్దమూ లేదు — ప్రశాంతంగా సాగిపోతోంది. రాముడు ప్రశాంతంగా, అడవి వైపు చూస్తూ, "ఈ అరణ్యం మాకు కొత్త ఇల్లు," అని అన్నారు. సీతమ్మ ముఖంలో ధైర్యం, ఆశ కనిపించాయి, లక్ష్మణుడు అప్రమత్తంగా చుట్టూ చూస్తున్నాడు. వెనక్కి తిరిగి చూశారు — ఒక కొత్త జీవితం కోసం. దూరమవుతున్న అయోధ్య దీపాలను చూసి, వారికి వీడ్కోలు పలికారు.
scenes.image_prompt: The three characters sit in a small boat, silently crossing the river, with only the gentle lapping of water against the hull. They look back at the receding lights of Ayodhya, bidding farewell to their former home. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Twilight over the wide river, soft blues and purples blending at the horizon, a sense of transition and the unknown ahead.
scenes.scene_visual: The three characters sit in a small boat, silently crossing the river, with only the gentle lapping of water against the hull. They look back at the receding lights of Ayodhya, bidding farewell to their former home.
scenes.text_te_en: Gangaa Nadi Odduku Cherukunna Raamudu, Sitamma, Lakshmanudu Oka Chinna Padavalo Nadini Daatadam Modalupettaaru. Aa Chinna Padavaku Niti Taakidi Tappa, Vere E Shabdamu Ledu — Prashaantangaa Saagipotondi. Raamudu Prashaantangaa, Adavi Vaipu Chustu, "I Aranyam Maaku Kotta Illu," Ani Annaaru. Sitamma Mukhanlo Dhairyan, Aasha Kanipinchaayi, Lakshmanudu Apramattangaa Chuttu Chustunnaadu. Venakki Tirigi Chushaaru — Oka Kotta Jivitam Kosan. Duramavutunna Ayodhya Dipaalanu Chusi, Vaariki Vidkolu Palikaaru.
scenes.text_en: Reaching the bank of the Ganga river, Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana began to cross the river in a small boat. There was no sound from the little boat except the gentle lapping of water — it moved along peacefully. Rama calmly looked towards the forest and said, "This forest is our new home." Courage and hope shone on Sita's face, while Lakshmana vigilantly looked around. They looked back — towards a new life. Seeing the distant lights of Ayodhya, they bid them farewell.

scenes.text: అదే సమయంలో, అయోధ్యలో, ప్రియమైన రాముని వియోగం తట్టుకోలేక దశరథ మహారాజు ప్రాణం విడిచారు. రాజభవనం కన్నీటి సంద్రంగా మారింది, రాణుల గుండెలు పగిలేలా ఏడుస్తున్నారు. దశరథుడు తన చివరి శ్వాసలో — రాముడి పేరే పలికాడు. ఆయన ముఖంలో ప్రశాంతమైన విముక్తి కనిపించింది, తన మాట నిలబెట్టుకున్నందుకు. అంతటా దుఃఖం నిండిపోయింది. ఈ విషాద వార్త భరతుడికి చేరడానికి, అతను అయోధ్యకు దూరంగా ఉన్నందున, సమయం పట్టింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The old king lies still, having passed away, while the queens around him are weeping uncontrollably. A peaceful, liberated expression rests on the king's face, even as the palace is filled with the sound of heartbroken wails. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Dasharatha — a well-known Hindu mythology figure, depicted accurately per canonical tradition. A solemn, almost grey light fills Dasaratha's private chambers, reflecting the deep mourning, hushed and reverent.
scenes.scene_visual: The old king lies still, having passed away, while the queens around him are weeping uncontrollably. A peaceful, liberated expression rests on the king's face, even as the palace is filled with the sound of heartbroken wails.
scenes.text_te_en: Ade Samayanlo, Ayodhyalo, Priyamaina Raamuni Viyogam Tattukoleka Dasharatha Mahaaraaju Praanam Vidichaaru. Raajabhavanam Kanniti Sandrangaa Maarindi, Raanula Gundelu Pagilelaa Edustunnaaru. Dasharathudu Tana Chivari Shvaasalo — Raamudi Pere Palikaadu. Aayana Mukhanlo Prashaantamaina Vimukti Kanipinchindi, Tana Maata Nilabettukunnanduku. Antataa Duhkham Nindipoyindi. I Vishaada Vaarta Bharatudiki Cheradaaniki, Atanu Ayodhyaku Durangaa Unnanduna, Samayam Pattindi.
scenes.text_en: At the same time, in Ayodhya, King Dasharatha passed away, unable to bear the separation from his beloved Rama. The royal palace became a sea of tears, and the queens wept heartbrokenly. Dasharatha, in his last breath — whispered Rama's name. A peaceful calm appeared on his face, for having kept his promise. Sorrow filled every corner. It took some time for this sad news to reach Bharata, as he was far away from Ayodhya.

scenes.text: అయోధ్యకు తిరిగి వచ్చిన భరతుడు, తన తండ్రి మరణ వార్త విని తీవ్ర దుఃఖంలో మునిగిపోయాడు. కానీ రాజ్యాన్ని స్వీకరించడానికి నిరాకరించాడు, తన అన్న రాముడికి మాత్రమే అది చెందాలని. భరతుడు రాముని పాదుకలను సింహాసనంపై పెట్టాడు — అవి ప్రకాశవంతంగా మెరుస్తూ కనిపించాయి. "రాముడే రాజు, నేను కేవలం సేవకుడిని," అని భరతుడు వినయంగా పలికారు. ఆ సింహాసనంపై — ధర్మం నిలిచింది. పద్నాలుగు సంవత్సరాలు రాముని ప్రతినిధిగా రాజ్యాన్ని పరిపాలించాడు, ఇచ్చిన మాటను గౌరవిస్తూ.
scenes.image_prompt: The brother, grief-stricken, places a pair of sandals onto the royal throne, refusing to sit on it himself. The sandals glow brightly on the empty throne, symbolizing the true king's presence. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Bharata: a young prince with a fair complexion, wearing a simple white dhoti and minimal ornaments (he lives in austerity while Rama is in exile). Gentle, grief-stricken yet dignified expression. Carries Rama's sandals (paduka) as a symbol of his brother's authority. A dignified, golden light illuminates the throne room, highlighting the sandals and Bharata's humble posture, symbolizing righteous rule and sacrifice.
scenes.scene_visual: The brother, grief-stricken, places a pair of sandals onto the royal throne, refusing to sit on it himself. The sandals glow brightly on the empty throne, symbolizing the true king's presence.
scenes.text_te_en: Ayodhyaku Tirigi Vachchina Bharatudu, Tana Tandri Marana Vaarta Vini Tivra Duhkhanlo Munigipoyaadu. Kaani Raajyaanni Svikarinchadaaniki Niraakarinchaadu, Tana Anna Raamudiki Maatrame Adi Chendaalani. Bharatudu Raamuni Paadukalanu Sinhaasanampai Pettaadu — Avi Prakaashavantangaa Merustu Kanipinchaayi. "Raamude Raaju, Nenu Kevalam Sevakudini," Ani Bharatudu Vinayangaa Palikaaru. Aa Sinhaasanampai — Dharmam Nilichindi. Padnaalugu Sanvatsaraalu Raamuni Pratinidhigaa Raajyaanni Paripaalinchaadu, Ichchina Maatanu Gauravistu.
scenes.text_en: When Bharata returned to Ayodhya, he was plunged into deep sorrow upon hearing the news of his father's death. But he refused to accept the kingdom, saying it belonged only to his elder brother Rama. Bharata placed Rama's sandals on the throne — they seemed to shine brightly. "Rama is the king, I am merely a servant," Bharata said humbly. On that throne — righteousness stood. He ruled the kingdom for fourteen years as Rama's representative, honoring the promise.

supporting_characters.description: Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Always the same character across every scene.

Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with jasmine flowers. Wearing a silk saree in deep red or golden hues, with traditional gold ornaments: necklace, bangles, nose ring, and earrings. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, composed expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Dasharatha: An elderly king with a regal white beard and distinguished features. Wearing rich royal silks and a jeweled crown, his face often shows the weight of his responsibilities and a deep love for his children. Always the same character across every scene.

Kaikeyi: A queen of Ayodhya with a beautiful but often stern countenance. Dressed in elegant royal garments, her expression can shift from regal to determined, reflecting her strong will and deep affection for her son Bharata. Always the same character across every scene.

Bharata: a young prince with a fair complexion, wearing a simple white dhoti and minimal ornaments (he lives in austerity while Rama is in exile). Gentle, grief-stricken yet dignified expression. Carries Rama's sandals (paduka) as a symbol of his brother's authority. Always the same character across every scene.

quality_scores.notes: This story boasts a compelling narrative arc and effectively conveys emotional depth through vivid imagery and character actions. The use of suspense hooks helps maintain engagement. However, the language often leans towards formal written prose, lacking the colloquial warmth and intimate tone of a true oral bedtime story. Additionally, the presence of an archaic grammatical form (-గలదు) detracts from the desired natural spoken Telugu. To elevate this to an award-worthy piece, the most crucial step is to infuse the narration with more conversational, grandmotherly Telugu, making it feel less like a report and more like a cherished tale being shared.

moral_te_en: Ichchina Maata Nilabettukovadame Goppa Dharmam Anipistundi, Adi Kashtamainaa Sare.
title_en: Father's Word — The Path of Righteousness
moral_en: Keeping one's promise is a great duty, even when it's difficult.
topic: దశరథుని సత్య వాక్పరిపాలన - ఇచ్చిన మాట కోసం రాముని త్యాగం
